chapter_title.ar: باب مَنْ تُكْرَهُ الصَّلَاةُ خَلْفَهُ وَ الْعَبْدِ يَؤُمُّ الْقَوْمَ وَ مَنْ أَحَقُّ أَنْ يُؤَمَّ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 51 – The one behind whom the Salāt is disliked, and the slave leading the people, and the one who is rightful of leading (the Salāt)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/259)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/259)
text: قَالَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( صلوات الله عليه ) لَا يَؤُمُّ الْمُقَيَّدُ الْمُطْلَقِينَ وَ لَا يَؤُمُّ صَاحِبُ الْفَالِجِ الْأَصِحَّاءَ وَ لَا صَاحِبُ التَّيَمُّمِ الْمُتَوَضِّينَ وَ لَا يَؤُمُّ الْأَعْمَى فِي الصَّحْرَاءِ إِلَّا أَنْ يُوَجَّهَ إِلَى الْقِبْلَةِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Al Nowfaly, from Al Sakuny,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The restricted cannot lead the free, nor can the paralysed lead the healthy, nor can the one with Tayammum lead the ones in ablution, nor can the blind lead in the desert except if he is directed towards the Qiblah’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from al-Nawfaliy from al-Sakuniy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that ’Amir al-Mu’minin said, ‘One bonded cannot lead in Salah (prayer) those who are free, one paralyzed cannot lead those in good health, one with Tayammum cannot lead those with Wudu’, a blind cannot lead others in the wilderness unless directed to al-Qiblah (al-Ka‘bah).’”
